title: SaaS Sales Call – US Rep × Tokyo Enterprise
description: A US sales rep (Jordan) pitching a project management SaaS to a Japanese enterprise buyer (Tanaka-san). Watch for indirect rejection signals, consensus language, and relationship-building patterns.

messages.text: Good morning, Tanaka-san! Thank you so much for making time today. I'm really excited to show you what our platform can do for your team.
Good morning, Jordan-san. Yes, we are happy to listen.
So I looked at your company's website and I think we can help you reduce project delays by 40%. Can I ask – what's your biggest bottleneck right now?
Hmm… that is an interesting question. We have many considerations at this stage.
messages.note: 🔴 Indirect deflection – they won't state problems directly

messages.text: I understand. Let me show you a quick demo of the dashboard. I think you'll find it very intuitive.
Yes, please go ahead.
Great! So here's the Kanban view – your team can drag tasks, set due dates, and tag dependencies. What do you think?
It is very… comprehensive. I see there are many features.
Yes exactly! And we can turn off any features you don't need. Would this work for your team?
We would need to consult with our team and understand the implications more carefully.
messages.note: 🔴 Consensus requirement – Japanese enterprise approval process

messages.text: Of course! How many stakeholders would be involved in this decision?
There are several departments. It is a process that takes time. We hope you understand.
messages.note: 🔴 Timeline softening – do not push for urgency

messages.text: Absolutely, I completely understand. Would it help if I prepared a detailed written proposal you could share internally?
messages.note: ✅ Good adaptation to Japanese decision process

messages.text: Yes, I think that would be very helpful. A written document would make it easier for everyone to review.
messages.note: 🟢 Positive signal – written materials are preferred

messages.text: Perfect. I'll send a full proposal with pricing tiers, case studies from similar companies, and an FAQ document. Does end of this week work?
Yes, that timing is acceptable.
Wonderful. And would it be all right if we scheduled a follow-up call next week? I want to make sure I can answer any questions your team may have.
Let me check with my colleagues and we will confirm.
messages.note: 🔴 'Let me check' = consensus required, don't push

messages.text: Of course – no rush at all. I'll include my direct contact in the proposal so anyone on your team can reach out anytime.
That is very considerate of you. Thank you, Jordan-san.
